question.text: In the context of the Black-Scholes model, N(x) represents:

answer.text: The standard normal cumulative distribution function

explanation.text: **The Logic:** N(x) is the standard normal CDF — it gives the probability that a normally distributed variable falls below x. It's used in Black-Scholes to calculate the probability-weighted expected payoff. **The Trap:** N(x) is a mathematical function, not a market term. **Cross-Exam Context:** Black-Scholes formula components are tested in Series I. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** N(d1) is approximately equal to the delta of the option. N(d2) relates to the probability of the option expiring ITM.
